vitals.date: UNVERIFIED — no standalone mass-participation event could be confirmed to exist
vitals.date_specific: These vitals could not be verified against any real, bookable mass-participation event as of 24 Jul 2026. See biased_opinion and catalog_flags: the figures below closely resemble the PROFESSIONAL Tour of Oman's Green Mountain summit-finish stage, not a documented amateur sportive.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 63. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 60. NOT re-scored during 2026-07-24 editorial wave 9 — dimensions/overall_score left as-is despite serious doubt about whether this profile describes a real, bookable event at all; see catalog_flags. A human re-scoring or deletion decision is recommended rather than a silent number change here.

course_description.character: IMPORTANT — this profile's identity could not be verified. Research (see catalog_flags) found the official Tour of Oman site (tour-of-oman.com) covering only the men's UCI ProSeries stage race and the professional Muscat Classic — no 'sportive,' 'gran fondo,' 'cyclo,' or amateur mass-participation ride tied to the Tour of Oman brand turned up in the sources checked. The 'Green Mountain' climb referenced below is a real public road and a real professional-race summit finish, but no documented amateur route using it was found; the 'Yates-style accelerations from pros' language in the existing suffering_zones is a strong tell that this text describes the PRO race, not a verified rideable public event.
course_description.signature_challenge: Unverified — this profile could not be confirmed to describe a real, bookable event distinct from the professional Tour of Oman stage race.
suffering_zones: Green Mountain climb (real, but professional-race-only): the Tour of Oman's actual summit finish is a genuine, famous, extremely steep climb (roughly 5.7km at double-digit average gradient) — but it is raced by the men's UCI ProSeries field, and no evidence supports a public amateur ride using this finish under a 'Tour of Oman Sportive' banner.
Coastal headwind exposure along Muscat's highways is plausible for any Oman road route, but is not tied to any documented amateur event here either.

history.origin_story: The professional Tour of Oman stage race held its first edition in 2010 (a UCI ProSeries event, not WorldTour). No founding date or history could be established for a separate 'Tour of Oman Sportive' mass-participation event — no evidence for one was found in this research.

biased_opinion.verdict: Could Not Verify This Event Exists
biased_opinion.summary: After research covering the official tour-of-oman.com site, its published regulations, and the third-party cycling calendars searched, no standalone 'Tour of Oman Sportive' mass-participation event could be found, distinct from the men's professional Tour of Oman stage race (UCI ProSeries, not WorldTour). None of the sources checked mention a public amateur ride, gran fondo, or cyclosportive under the Tour of Oman name — that's an absence of evidence in the sources reviewed, not proof no such event has ever existed. The course details previously on this profile (a 5.7km/10.4% 'Green Mountain' finish, references to 'Yates-style accelerations from pros ahead') match the professional race's actual Green Mountain summit-finish stage, not any documented amateur route — a strong sign this profile's content was generated by conflating pro-race coverage with an event that could not be separately verified. The closest genuinely real adjacent things found: a small pre-stage-1 'Oman Community Race' (20km race plus a 2.7km family parade, reported at hundreds of participants, no durable registration page, not a gran fondo), and the unrelated Wadi Dayqah Challenge (a distinct, unaffiliated long-distance non-competitive Omani ride, 140/240km, with an active registration page as of this research).
strengths: The Green Mountain climb this profile references is a real public road and a genuine, steep professional race finish — the road itself isn't in question, only its use in any public sportive under this name
weaknesses: No standalone 'Tour of Oman Sportive' could be found in the official Tour of Oman materials or independent cycling calendars checked in this research

The course description previously on file (Green Mountain finish, pro-rider references) matches the professional race, not any documented public event — a conflation risk, not a verified account of a rideable amateur route
No registration link, field size, entry fee, or organizer contact could be found for anything matching this profile's name and vitals

If a rider actually wants to ride in Oman, the real options found (Oman Community Race — a short community ride, not a gran fondo; or the unrelated Wadi Dayqah Challenge) are materially different in distance, format, and prestige from what this profile claims
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Do not treat this as a bookable event. Nothing found in this research supports a real 'Tour of Oman Sportive' distinct from the professional stage race. If you want to ride in Oman, look at the Wadi Dayqah Challenge (a distinct, unaffiliated, registrable event) instead — this profile's data appears to be a conflation of professional race coverage, not a verified amateur event.

final_verdict.one_liner: Could not verify this event exists — likely conflated with the professional Tour of Oman's Green Mountain stage.
final_verdict.should_you_race: No — no evidence supports a real, bookable 'Tour of Oman Sportive' separate from the professional stage race. Do not plan travel around this listing.
final_verdict.alternatives: If you want a real, registrable long-distance ride in Oman, look at the Wadi Dayqah Challenge (140km/240km non-competitive options, muscatbike.com) — a genuine, unaffiliated event with an actual annual registration page.

catalog_flags.status_note: IDENTITY/EXISTENCE PROBLEM: no standalone 'Tour of Oman Sportive' mass-participation event could be found in the sources checked — official tour-of-oman.com covers only the men's professional UCI ProSeries race and the professional Muscat Classic, with no sportive/gran fondo/cyclo/amateur content found there. This profile's course description (Green Mountain 5.7km/10.4% finish, 'Yates-style accelerations from pros ahead') matches the PROFESSIONAL race's actual Green Mountain summit stage, suggesting this profile conflates pro-race coverage with an event that could not be separately verified (not proof no such event has ever existed — absence of evidence, not evidence of absence). Closest real adjacent things found: the small Oman Community Race (20km + family parade, not a gran fondo, no durable registration) and the unrelated Wadi Dayqah Challenge (a distinct, unaffiliated, registrable event). Recommend a human decide whether to delete this profile entirely, recast it honestly around the Oman Community Race with drastically different vitals, or keep it flagged pending further research — this is a stronger case for removal than the flandrien-ride precedent, because no clearly-identifiable real substitute event was found at all.

eligibility.notes: No standalone 'Tour of Oman Sportive' mass-participation event could be verified to exist as of 24 Jul 2026. Official tour-of-oman.com and its published regulations cover only the men's UCI ProSeries stage race (not WorldTour) and the professional Muscat Classic; searches for sportive/gran fondo/cyclo/amateur content tied to the Tour of Oman brand found nothing. This profile's course details closely match the professional race's Green Mountain summit-finish stage, suggesting conflation rather than a genuine distinct amateur event. 'Unknown' reflects that no verifiable event of this description could be found, not confirmation that one exists in some active or dormant state. For context (not this profile's subject): the professional Tour of Oman itself ran 7-11 Feb 2026 (Christian Scaroni won), having previously been cancelled in 2020 (national mourning) and postponed/never rescheduled in 2021 (COVID).
